appSubtitle: View and manage your filament spools
rfidNotFound: RFID scanned: {{uid}} — spool not found in current inventory.
td1sNotFound: TD1S scan: {{td}} — spool not found in inventory.
stgDataHelp: Export your inventory locally as JSON, or copy the public API URL (with your access key embedded) so a script or another machine can fetch it remotely.
stgCopyApiUrlOk: Copied — keep this URL private; anyone with it can read your inventory.
stgCopyApiUrlNoKey: Access key not loaded yet — try again in a moment.
addAccountAuthError: Invalid email or password. Please check your credentials.
confirmDeleteAccount: Remove this account and all its local data?
delModalWarn: This account will be removed from this device. You can reconnect at any time.
rawScaleHint: Weigh spool + container on the scale — the server subtracts the container weight.
twinLinkPickerSub: Same brand, material, type and colour as this spool.
loginResetSent: Check your inbox — reset link sent!
loginPasswordTooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters.
tdEditConnectMsg: Connect your TD1S sensor via USB and remove filament
tdConnectMsg: Connect your TD1S sensor via USB
invEmptyTitle: Time to scan your filament spools!
invEmptyText: Grab the TigerTag app, scan your spools and watch your inventory come to life. It takes 10 seconds per spool — we timed it.
setupNameSub: Your display name is visible in the app and to your friends!
invWelcomeTitle: Manage your inventory from your phone.
invWelcomeSub: Scan your TigerTag spools in 10 seconds. Your inventory updates in real time.
invQrScanHint: Scan QR code to install on your mobile phone
friendNotFound: No user found with this code.
friendRegenConfirm: Regenerate private key? Current friends keep their access. Old links are revoked.
friendsEmptySub: Add a friend by their code to see their inventory.
friendInvErrorHint: Your friend may have changed their access key. Ask them to remove and re-add you, or to enable public mode.
friendNotSharing: This user does not wish to share their inventory.
racksEmptySub: Create a rack to organise your spool storage.
racksEmptyFriendSub: This user hasn't set up any racks yet.
rackEditSub: Pick a preset or create a custom layout.
rackDeleteConfirm: Delete the rack "{{name}}"? Spools assigned to it will be unassigned.
printersEmptySub: Add your 3D printers from the TigerTag mobile app — they'll appear here automatically.
printersEmptyBullet1: Bambu Lab, Creality, Elegoo, FlashForge or Snapmaker
printersEmptyBullet2: Sync over your local network — credentials never leave your account
printersEmptyBullet3: Pick the active printer for each spool when you scan a tag
printersFriendNASub: A friend's 3D printers are owner-only — they're not visible from this view.
printerAddPickSub: Choose the manufacturer of your 3D printer
snapNoConnection: Open this view while the printer is on your network to see live status.
snapLogEmpty: No traffic yet — open the panel while the printer is online.
snapFilEditableTip: Click to edit this filament manually
snapFilLockedTip: Read-only — RFID tag detected by the printer
snapFilEditError: Send failed — check the printer's WebSocket.
printerAddErrName: Please give your printer a name.
printerAddErrSave: Couldn't save. Check your network and try again.
printerHelperBambuBullets: Open Settings → General → LAN Only Mode on your printer.
Note the IP, Serial Number and 8-digit Access Code shown on screen.
TigerTag connects over MQTT on your local network.
printerHelperCrealityBullets: Find the IP in Network → Printer Information.
Use your Klipper / Moonraker access password.
printerHelperElegooBullets: Find the IP and Serial Number in Settings → Network.
MQTT password is optional — leave empty to use the factory default.
printerHelperFFGBullets: Find the IP and Serial in About → Network.
Use the password you set in FlashPrint or Orca-Flashforge.
printerHelperSnapBullets: Just enter the local IP shown on the Snapmaker network screen.
printerHintBambuIP: Local IP shown in LAN Only Mode.
printerHintBambuCode: 8-digit code from the LAN Only Mode screen.
printerHintBambuSerial: Listed below the access code on the same screen.
printerHintElegooSerial: Serial number from the printer's About screen.
printerHintElegooMqtt: Leave empty to use the factory default.
printerHintFFGSerial: Serial number from the printer's About screen.
printerHintFFGPassword: Same password you use in FlashPrint.
printerHintSnapIP: Local IP shown on the Snapmaker network screen.
snapScanEmpty: No Snapmaker found. Check it's powered on and on the same Wi-Fi.
snapManualSub: Type the printer's local IP — we'll probe it to pre-fill the rest.
snapScanNoSubnets: No network detected. Connect to Wi-Fi.
snapScanExtraSubnetsHint: Add another network if your printer is elsewhere (e.g. 192.168.40).
snapScanEmptyFirewall: No reply on {{p}}. Use "Add by IP" with the exact address.
printerSecretWarn: These secrets grant LAN-level access to your printer. Don't share them or paste into untrusted apps.
errReportSub: Copy this report and send it to support so we can help.
rackAutoFillTip: Place unranked spools into empty unlocked slots
rackLockedTip: Locked · right-click a slot to lock/unlock
storageAutoAssignTip: Place in the first available slot
autoUnstorageSub: Free the rack slot when a spool reaches 0g
rackStatsDepletedTip: Active spools with no filament left (weight 0g)
dbgDeletedPurgeConfirm: Permanently delete "{{name}}" from the database? This cannot be undone.
dbgDeletedPurgeConfirmTwin: Permanently delete "{{name}}" AND its linked twin tag from the database? This cannot be undone.
aboutAutoUpdateSub: Check for new versions on startup
rackEmptyConfirm: Empty rack "{{name}}"? All assigned spools will be unassigned (the rack itself stays).
scaleRemoveConfirm: Remove "{{name}}" from the list? It will reappear automatically on next heartbeat.
scaleEmptyTitle: Add a TigerScale to your setup
scaleEmptySub: Place a spool on the scale — it's automatically detected, weighed, and synced with your inventory. Zero clicks.
scaleEmptyBullet1: Automatically detects the RFID and identifies your spool
scaleEmptyBullet2: Subtracts the container weight so you see how much filament is really available
scaleEmptyBullet3: Fully hands-free — no app to open, no button to press
rackClearAllConfirm: Clear all spools from "{{name}}"? They will be returned to "Unranked spools" (the rack stays).
spoolMarkDeletedTip: Hold 1.5s to remove this spool from your inventory (it will appear in Settings → Debug → Deleted, restorable from there)
uidMigrConfirmMsg: {{count}} spools in your inventory use the legacy storage format. Upgrading converts them to the new format. Estimated time: about {{duration}} — please do not close the app while it's running.
uidMigrProgressMsg: Your inventory is being upgraded to a new, more efficient storage format. Please do not close the app until this finishes. Each spool only takes a fraction of a second.
uidMigrProgressWarn: ⚠ Closing the app now would just resume on next launch — but for the cleanest experience, let it finish.
rackMigrConfirmMsg: We found {{count}} spool(s) using the legacy storage layout. Upgrading regroups the rack location into a single tidy field — takes {{duration}}, runs in the background, and your spools stay where they are.
rackMigrProgressMsg: Your storage assignments are being repackaged. Please don't close the app — each spool only takes a fraction of a second.
rackMigrProgressWarn: ⚠ Closing the app now would just resume on next launch — but for the cleanest experience, let it finish.
migrationConfirmMsg: A small data update is ready for {{count}} item(s). It runs in the background and takes {{duration}}. Your data stays exactly where it is — nothing will move or get lost.
uidMigrConfirmMobileWarn: Before continuing, make sure your mobile app is updated to version 1.0.3 or later.
migrationProgressMsg: Tidying up your data — please don't close the app. Each item only takes a fraction of a second.
migrationProgressWarn: ⚠ Closing now would just resume on next launch — but for the cleanest experience, let it finish.
ffgErrSnMismatch: Bad serial number — please check printer settings
ffgErrBadPassword: Bad password — please check printer settings
